PETITIONER'S COUNSEL DID NOT SERVE AND FILE THE NOTICE OF HEARING FOR PERITION FOR DECLARATORY RELIEF AT LEAST 16 COURT DAYS BEFORE THE HEARING, AS REQUIRED BY CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 1005(B). PETITIONER MAY RE-FILE THE MOTION. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR., CLERK: M. GOODMAN, NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

calendar.matters:
Petition For Declaratory Relief
Off Calendar Sep-01-2022 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: PETITIONER NICHOLAS CASAGRANDE's Petition For Declaratory Relief.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Nicholas Casagrande's petition for declaratory relief is granted.

Upon review of all evidence submitted, the Court finds the documents listed do in fact refer to the same person, John August Pedroli. Mr. John Pedroli and Ms. Kathleen Janice Pedroli are petitioner's direct ascendants. Mr. John Pedroli's birth certificate and Ms. Kathleen Janice Pedroli's birth certificate misstate Mr. John Pedroli's middle name, which is in fact August. John Angelo Pedroli, John August Pedroli, and John Augusto Pedroli as described in the certified records provided by petitioner are one and the same person.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

Notice Of Hearing For Petition For Declaratory Relief
Off Calendar Jul-28-2022 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows: PETITIONER NICHOLAS CASAGRANDE's Petition For Declaratory Relief. Off calendar. Petitioner's counsel did not serve and file the notice of hearing for perition for declaratory relief at least 16 court days before the hearing, as required by Code of Civil Procedure section 1005(b). Petitioner may re-file the motion. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/RBU)
